description_long: This imposing castle is constructed from reddish-brown stone blocks and features four prominent square towers at each corner, each topped with a hipped, tiled roof. The main structure is rectangular, with a central courtyard visible from above, and the walls are punctuated by rows of arched windows. The roof is covered in weathered terracotta tiles, giving the structure an aged, historic appearance. Decorative corbel tables run beneath the roofline, and the main entrance is marked by a large arched doorway with stone detailing. The towers are slightly taller than the main building, providing elevated vantage points. The overall design is reminiscent of fortified Renaissance or late Medieval European castles, blending defensive and residential architectural elements.
description: A large, rectangular stone castle with four corner towers, tiled roofs, and arched windows, featuring a central courtyard and a grand arched entrance.
description_view_0: Front-facing view showing two tall corner towers, the main arched entrance, and rows of arched windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the four towers, and the central open courtyard.
description_view_2: Rear view with two towers and a less ornate wall, showing the tiled roof and window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the symmetry of the towers and the open courtyard.
